Comment on PR #723 by chatgpt-codex-connector[bot]: **<sub><sub>![P2 Badge](https://img.shields.io/badge/P2-yellow?style=flat)</sub></sub>  Keep life-map worker ID stable for existing conversations**
body:
**<sub><sub>![P2 Badge](https://img.shields.io/badge/P2-yellow?style=flat)</sub></sub>  Keep life-map worker ID stable for existing conversations**

Changing the life-map worker ID to `life-map-attendant` creates a brand-new worker record instead of updating existing `life-map-mesa` records in user stores. Existing room conversations are loaded by `roomId` and retain their old `workerId`, and server message processing still looks up workers via `conversation.workerId`, so historical conversations can continue to run against stale Mesa identity/state while the UI reads a different inactive worker. This breaks lifecycle compatibility for existing data unless you keep the same worker ID (or add a migration that rewrites old conversation/worker references).

Useful? React with 👍 / 👎.

text: Comment on PR #724 by chatgpt-codex-connector[bot]: **<sub><sub>![P1 Badge](https://img.shields.io/badge/P1-orange?style=flat)</sub></sub>  Preserve storeId on legacy sorting-room redirect**
body:
**<sub><sub>![P1 Badge](https://img.shields.io/badge/P1-orange?style=flat)</sub></sub>  Preserve storeId on legacy sorting-room redirect**

The new `/sorting-room/*` redirect drops the current query string by navigating to `ROUTES.LIFE_MAP` as a plain pathname, so URLs like `/sorting-room?storeId=<instance>` lose their instance context during migration. In this app, missing `storeId` is then repopulated from `determineStoreIdFromUser` in `EnsureStoreId`, which can switch users with multiple instances to a different workspace than the one in the original link, leading them to view/edit the wrong data set.

Useful? React with 👍 / 👎.

The protected route table now skips `ROUTES.SORTING_ROOM` and `ROUTES.SORTING_ROOM_STREAM`, so `/sorting-room` URLs no longer match any app route after auth and users cannot access the prioritization workflow (including existing bookmarks/deep links). This commit keeps Sorting Room code and room definitions elsewhere, so removing these route entries introduces a regression unrelated to drafting-room removal.

text: Comment on PR #724 by chatgpt-codex-connector[bot]: **<sub><sub>![P1 Badge](https://img.shields.io/badge/P1-orange?style=flat)</sub></sub>  Preserve storeId when redirecting legacy sorting URLs**
body:
**<sub><sub>![P1 Badge](https://img.shields.io/badge/P1-orange?style=flat)</sub></sub>  Preserve storeId when redirecting legacy sorting URLs**

The new legacy redirect sends every `/sorting-room` request to `/life-map` without query params, so links like `/sorting-room?storeId=<instance>` silently drop the instance context. In multi-instance accounts this can switch users into their default instance after `EnsureStoreId` runs, which means bookmarked/shared sorting-room URLs now open the wrong data set. Please carry `location.search` (at least `storeId`) through this redirect.

Useful? React with 👍 / 👎.

Comment on PR #728 by chatgpt-codex-connector[bot]: **<sub><sub>![P1 Badge](https://img.shields.io/badge/P1-orange?style=flat)</sub></sub>  Ignore handled Escape events before closing overlay**
body:
**<sub><sub>![P1 Badge](https://img.shields.io/badge/P1-orange?style=flat)</sub></sub>  Ignore handled Escape events before closing overlay**

This global Escape handler closes the entire overlay even when a nested dialog has already handled Escape. In `/projects/:projectId`, `TaskDetailModal` listens for Escape to close or exit edit mode, but that key event bubbles to `window` and triggers `onClose()` here too, so users get kicked back to the map instead of just dismissing the task modal. Please guard this with `event.defaultPrevented` (or equivalent propagation control) so overlay close only runs when Escape was not consumed by inner UI.

Useful? React with 👍 / 👎.

text: Comment on PR #728 by chatgpt-codex-connector[bot]: **<sub><sub>![P1 Badge](https://img.shields.io/badge/P1-orange?style=flat)</sub></sub>  Restore project-specific RoomLayout for project overlay**
body:
**<sub><sub>![P1 Badge](https://img.shields.io/badge/P1-orange?style=flat)</sub></sub>  Restore project-specific RoomLayout for project overlay**

Wrapping the project overlay under `RoomLayout room={LIFE_MAP_ROOM}` means project pages no longer run inside a project room context, because `ProjectDetailPage` also stopped rendering its own `RoomLayout`. As a result, opening chat from a project overlay binds to the life-map conversation/agent instead of the project-specific one, which changes room-scoped chat behavior for `/projects/:projectId`. The project overlay route needs to preserve a project room-backed layout/context.

Useful? React with 👍 / 👎.

Comment on PR #729 by chatgpt-codex-connector[bot]: **<sub><sub>![P2 Badge](https://img.shields.io/badge/P2-yellow?style=flat)</sub></sub>  Disable global arrow-key capture when overlays are open**
body:
**<sub><sub>![P2 Badge](https://img.shields.io/badge/P2-yellow?style=flat)</sub></sub>  Disable global arrow-key capture when overlays are open**

`CameraRig` installs a `keydown` handler on `window` and later calls `event.preventDefault()` for every arrow key press, so arrow keys are consumed even when focus is on non-editable overlay UI instead of the map. On sanctuary/workshop routes this causes the background map to pan while the foreground `BuildingOverlay` panel (which is scrollable) can no longer use arrow-key scrolling/navigation; camera controls should be scoped to map focus or disabled when overlay state is active.

Useful? React with 👍 / 👎.

That bypasses the event/query path the UI depends on in production, so Storybook can drift from real behavior (for example, if unplaced-project materialization regresses) while still appearing healthy; this should be wired through a LiveStore-backed story setup instead.

This effect freezes `showFirstOpenMarvinBanner` after the first settings load by guarding with `hasInitializedFirstOpenRef`, so the banner can stay visible for later project navigations in the same `/projects/:projectId` route instance even after `v1.SettingUpdated` marks the intro complete. In practice, a first-time user can see the “first-open” banner on subsequent projects during the same session because the state is never recalculated once initialized.

text: Comment on PR #738 by chatgpt-codex-connector[bot]: **<sub><sub>![P2 Badge](https://img.shields.io/badge/P2-yellow?style=flat)</sub></sub>  Delay first-visit completion until bootstrap message is delivered**
body:
**<sub><sub>![P2 Badge](https://img.shields.io/badge/P2-yellow?style=flat)</sub></sub>  Delay first-visit completion until bootstrap message is delivered**

This marks the Sanctuary journey as completed immediately, before the queued bootstrap prompt is actually sent to Jarvis. In this patch, delivery only happens later in `AttendantChatPanel` when `sendDirectMessage(...)` returns true (it returns false until worker/conversation readiness), so if the user leaves or reloads before that point, the in-memory queued message is lost but the persisted first-visit flag remains set. That causes subsequent visits to skip the onboarding prompt entirely.

Useful? React with 👍 / 👎.

Comment on PR #739 by chatgpt-codex-connector[bot]: **<sub><sub>![P1 Badge](https://img.shields.io/badge/P1-orange?style=flat)</sub></sub>  Recheck completion setting on later query updates**
body:
**<sub><sub>![P1 Badge](https://img.shields.io/badge/P1-orange?style=flat)</sub></sub>  Recheck completion setting on later query updates**

The first-visit decision is permanently locked on the first non-`undefined` query result because `resolvedFirstVisitRef` is set before later updates can be observed. If the setting query initially resolves to an empty array (e.g., before remote sync catches up in a fresh session/device) and then later receives the persisted completion row, this hook still treats the user as first-visit, sends the bootstrap prompt, and rewrites the completion timestamp incorrectly. The guard should allow reevaluation until a definitive completion value is observed.

Useful? React with 👍 / 👎.

body:
**<sub><sub>![P2 Badge](https://img.shields.io/badge/P2-yellow?style=flat)</sub></sub>  Gate onboarding UI policy on readiness**

When the onboarding setting is missing, this fallback immediately treats state as `completed` even while bootstrapping, so downstream UI (for example `NewUiShell` visibility gates) can briefly render the full rail/task queue before the campfire state is persisted and observed. On slower startup this creates a visible flash and momentary access to controls that onboarding is supposed to hide for first-time users.

Useful? React with 👍 / 👎.

text: Comment on PR #740 by chatgpt-codex-connector[bot]: **<sub><sub>![P2 Badge](https://img.shields.io/badge/P2-yellow?style=flat)</sub></sub>  Include archived projects in bootstrap activity detection**
body:
**<sub><sub>![P2 Badge](https://img.shields.io/badge/P2-yellow?style=flat)</sub></sub>  Include archived projects in bootstrap activity detection**

This activity check uses `projects.length`, but `projects` comes from `getProjects$`, which filters out archived records (`archivedAt: null` in `packages/shared/src/livestore/queries.ts`). In existing stores whose only history is archived projects (and no current project placements), bootstrap will incorrectly write a campfire state and force returning users through onboarding again.

Useful? React with 👍 / 👎.

Comment on PR #741 by chatgpt-codex-connector[bot]: **<sub><sub>![P1 Badge](https://img.shields.io/badge/P1-orange?style=flat)</sub></sub>  Remove client-time gate from first-project detection**
body:
**<sub><sub>![P1 Badge](https://img.shields.io/badge/P1-orange?style=flat)</sub></sub>  Remove client-time gate from first-project detection**

The onboarding promotion logic currently discards any project whose `createdAt` is earlier than `persistedState.startedAt`, but `startedAt` is written on the client while AI tool calls create projects on the server (`createProjectCore` uses server `new Date()`). If a user device clock is ahead (or server clock is behind), a valid first project from Campfire is filtered out, so onboarding never transitions out of `campfire` even after project/task creation succeeds.

Useful? React with 👍 / 👎.
